subject: Project Management
title: Story Points and Estimation
description: A micro-lesson that makes Story Points and Estimation usable.

hook.text: Scrum teams don't estimate in hours. They use 'story points' — a relative measure of effort.

buildup.text: A 2-point story is twice as hard as a 1-point story. A 5-point story is a big one.

discovery.text: Teams play 'Planning Poker' — everyone reveals their estimate simultaneously to avoid bias.

twist.text: Story points were meant to aid conversation. Many organizations use them to measure productivity.

climax.text: When points become targets, teams inflate them. The metric becomes meaningless.

punchline.text: Measure understanding, not output. That was the point.

quiz.question: Why do Scrum teams use story points instead of hours?
options: To measure relative effort and spark team conversation
Because hours are forbidden in Agile
To make reporting easier for managers
